steps.title: Find the sale price using the discount
steps.content:
When the sale price is 70% less than the regular price, it equals 30% of the regular price (since 100% - 70% = 30%).
$$\text{Sale price} = 0.30 \times 18.20 = 5.46$$

steps.title: Use the cost-to-sale relationship and solve for cost
steps.content:
We're told the sale price is 40% greater than the store's cost. This means:
$$\text{Sale price} = \text{Cost} \times 1.40$$
Substituting the sale price we found:
$$5.46 = \text{Cost} \times 1.40$$
Dividing both sides by 1.40:
$$\text{Cost} = \frac{5.46}{1.40} = 3.90$$
The store's cost for the backpack is $3.90, so the answer is 3.9.
